title: 2030 Alps Games already facing storm of internal controversies and public backlash
subhead: France received the Olympic flag on Sunday during the closing ceremony of the Milan-Cortina Winter Games. Behind the scenes, however, the handover has been anything but smooth, with multiple crises brewing and public resistance on the rise.

title: Death of Fredrick Brennan, creator of 8chan forum where QAnon emerged
Top Mexican drug cartel leader killed
Understanding the battle of Verdun through rare or little-known photographs
subhead: A book published to mark the 110th anniversary of the legendary battle brings to light many images taken by army photographers. Le Monde is presenting several of these photographs, offering a glimpse into the reality behind this symbol of the First World War.

title: Susan George, French-American political scientist and figure of the alter-globalization movement, dies
subhead: The essayist, co-founder of Attac, notably raised awareness among the French and Europeans about the damage caused by globalization. She died on February 14 at the age of 91.

title: Hong Kong appeals court upholds prison sentences for 12 pro-democracy activists
subhead: The Hong Kong opposition figures had their appeals dismissed, with their sentences ranging from four years and two months to 10 years, in a ruling that highlights the severity of Beijing's control over Hong Kong's politics.

title: Ukraine's Western allies push to drag the war out
subhead: All while sending weapons and declaring their support for Kyiv, Europeans and Americans have been tempted to play for time rather than push for a ceasefire. A truce would force them to deploy ground troops, and so they continue to rely on a war of attrition, leaving the Ukrainians on the front lines against the Russians.

Oleksandr Syrsky, commander of the Ukrainian army, to Le Monde: 'Russian army losses have exceeded its recruitment level'
subhead: The Ukrainian commander-in-chief told Le Monde that, despite a 'very difficult' situation on the battlefield, his army continued to resist 'the most powerful army in Europe' and to 'foil' Russian military plans.

title: AI has arrived in France's local elections – for better or worse
subhead: From creating campaign videos to writing speeches, artificial intelligence has become an effective tool for candidates. However, it also raises ethical questions, prompting some elected officials to call for regulation.

title: 2026 Winter Olympics: New infrastructure raises financial and environmental questions
subhead: The anticipated increase in visitor numbers to the protected Alpine regions is the first major impact expected from the Games. Critics have condemned what they call the 'gentrification of the mountains.'
